title: Fear Garden — Chapter 1: The Planter Fall
description: Chapter 1 of the Fear Garden saga. What looks like a single courtyard accident becomes the first loose root in a larger Beer Garden cover-up.

answerKey.method: Loosened the hanging planter rig above the courtyard stage
answerKey.motive: To stop Carter from exposing her stolen charity raffle money
answerKey.keyEvidence: Planter rig photo, raffle cash spreadsheet, and maya’s staff-key log
answerKey.explanation: Maya Bell had the access, timing, and motive. The proof path combines planter rig photo, raffle cash spreadsheet, and Maya’s staff-key log with the failed statement: Maya said she never entered storage. The planter fall reveals the first hidden thread: missing fundraiser cash and a reference to the Root Ledger.
answerKey.sagaConnection: The planter fall reveals the first hidden thread: missing fundraiser cash and a reference to the Root Ledger.
answerKey.chapterRevealHook: Carter solved the small theft, but his notes hint at something larger: the Root Ledger was not Maya’s invention. It was already waiting in the garden.

phone.title: Phone Evidence 1
phone.text: Carter Reed missed two calls from Maya Bell before the garden music cut out. This clue belongs to Chapter 1: The Planter Fall.

phone.title: Phone Evidence 2
phone.text: A final call lasted eleven seconds as Carter moved toward the side corridor.

messages.title: Messages Evidence 1
messages.text: Carter texted Maya: “We talk tonight. I saw the numbers.” This clue belongs to Chapter 1: The Planter Fall.

messages.title: Messages Evidence 2
messages.text: Maya replied: “Not at the bar. Meet by the garden gate.”

maps.title: Maps Evidence 1
maps.text: Carter’s phone pinged near the patio garden path at 8:07 PM. This clue belongs to Chapter 1: The Planter Fall.

maps.title: Maps Evidence 2
maps.text: Maya’s device appears nearby during the same short window.

bank.title: Bank Evidence 1
bank.text: A Beer Garden event ledger shows a suspicious private payout connected to stop carter from exposing her stolen charity raffle money. This clue belongs to Chapter 1: The Planter Fall.

bank.title: Bank Evidence 2
bank.text: The payout trail links to a device or account controlled by Maya. It also brushes against the Root Ledger thread running through the full Fear Garden story.

photos.title: Photos Evidence 1
photos.text: A guest photo shows Maya near the restricted service path before the incident. This clue belongs to Chapter 1: The Planter Fall.

photos.title: Photos Evidence 2
photos.text: Another photo captures the scene detail: loose planter rig over Carter’s table.

social.title: Social Evidence 1
social.text: A patio livestream catches a missing gap just before Carter disappears from frame. This clue belongs to Chapter 1: The Planter Fall.

social.title: Social Evidence 2
social.text: The posted version removes the seconds that would show who crossed behind the garden wall.

contacts.title: Contacts Evidence 1
contacts.text: Maya saved a contact under “Garden Services,” matching a suspicious payment line. This clue belongs to Chapter 1: The Planter Fall.

contacts.title: Contacts Evidence 2
contacts.text: Carter saved the same number with the note “ask about fake paperwork.”

notes.title: Notes Evidence 1
notes.text: Detective note: the killer needed venue access, timing, and a reason to silence Carter. This clue belongs to Chapter 1: The Planter Fall.

notes.title: Notes Evidence 2
notes.text: Detective note: the scene points to planning, not a random Beer Garden accident. It also brushes against the Root Ledger thread running through the full Fear Garden story.

files.title: Files Evidence 1

files.title: Files Evidence 2

browser.title: Browser Evidence 2

openingNarration: Detectives, welcome back to Fear Garden at Mid City Beer Garden in Baton Rouge, Louisiana. This is Chapter 1: The Planter Fall, built for Training Detective. Fear Garden is no longer a set of separate cases. It is one continuing story that grows darker and more elaborate with every chapter. This is the beginning of the Fear Garden story. No one knows yet that Carter’s death is the first visible crack in a deeper scheme. Tonight's investigation focuses on Carter Reed. The detectives learn the rules of the investigation while uncovering the first sign that money from charity nights was being moved through more than one set of hands. Study every app carefully. Earlier details may return with new meaning, and the Root Ledger thread is now part of the case foundation.
revealScript: Case revealed. Chapter 1: The Planter Fall is closed. The culprit was Maya Bell. The method: Loosened the hanging planter rig above the courtyard stage. The motive: To stop Carter from exposing her stolen charity raffle money. The proof: Planter rig photo, raffle cash spreadsheet, and maya’s staff-key log. Carter solved the small theft, but his notes hint at something larger: the Root Ledger was not Maya’s invention. It was already waiting in the garden.

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 1: What makes Carter’s death suspicious?

options.label: A normal spilled pint near the stage
loose planter rig over Carter’s table
A table number written backward
A guest closing a patio umbrella

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 2: Which timeline clue matters most?

options.label: The DJ restarted a playlist
A guest paid with cash
Maya’s key opened the storage closet before the fall

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 3: Which digital clue suggests planning?

options.label: A low battery warning
A misspelled hashtag
A normal Wi-Fi reconnect
a raffle cash spreadsheet missing two deposits

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 4: What clue points toward motive?

options.label: Maya’s apron fiber caught on the planter hook
A birthday receipt
A lost wristband
An ordinary tab transfer

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 5: Which statement begins to collapse?

options.label: Maya said the plants were watered
Maya said she never entered storage
Janelle said the gate was busy
Darius said the speaker was loud

Janelle Price
Maya Bell
Darius Cole

questions.prompt: Final 2: How was the murder carried out?

options.label: Poisoned a shared punch bowl
Cut the stage lights only

To stop Carter from exposing her stolen charity raffle money
To win a trivia prize

sagaTitle: Fear Garden: The Root Ledger Saga
storyArcSummary: The detectives learn the rules of the investigation while uncovering the first sign that money from charity nights was being moved through more than one set of hands.
previousChapterConnection: This is the beginning of the Fear Garden story. No one knows yet that Carter’s death is the first visible crack in a deeper scheme.
nextChapterHook: The stolen raffle money points to a phrase written beside old receipts: ROOT LEDGER.
